meta.title: Mercedes-Benz M260/M264 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.tagPill: Mercedes-Benz • M260/M264 • Petrol MHEV • 1.5/2.0L Turbo I4 • 2017–present
hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M260/M264 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The M260 and M264 are Mercedes' modern turbocharged four-cylinder MHEV engines — technologically advanced but cylinder head failures are a documented weak point. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [MB‑VERIFIED].

rows.value: 10+ (A, B, CLA, GLA, GLB, C, E, CLS, GLC, G)
Confidence: 85% confidence — based on cross-referenced specialist reports, owner forums, and documented warranty extensions; proprietary enquiry data pending full verification.
Reconditioned supply midpoint ~£5,000–£6,000 → falls in £5,000–£6,999 band

rows.metric: Cylinder head failure often requires full head replacement (not rebuild) — major, specialist-only work

rows.value: Available but cylinder heads are specialist-order items; some wait times

rows.metric: Depends on host vehicle value — see R-to-V flag guidance

rows.value: Sum of the five rows above

rows.metric: One-line verdict: The M260/M264 are refined, efficient modern fours — but cylinder head and timing chain failures are well-documented and expensive to fix — buy only with full service history and warranty cover.
rows.value: Best For: Drivers who want a modern, efficient Mercedes with the latest technology and are prepared for specialist maintenance.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Cross-link: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M260/M264 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [MB‑QUOTE]. A cylinder head replacement alone is a major repair requiring specialist work.
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand cost comparison applies.

cta.label: → Compare M260/M264 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: How much does an M260/M264 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned M260/M264 typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only [MB‑QUOTE], varying by displacement (1.5L vs 2.0L) and variant. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates.

items.question: Is the M260/M264 a reliable engine?
items.answer: The M260/M264 are considered reasonably reliable in early production, earning 3.0★ out of 5 [MB‑VERIFIED] — but well-documented cylinder head failures and timing chain issues mean diligent maintenance is essential.

items.question: What is the most common M260/M264 failure?
items.answer: Cylinder head failure — specifically exhaust valve seat wear leading to misfires and compression loss — typically appears between 40,000–120,000 miles. Mercedes issued extended warranties for the M260 in some markets.

items.question: Which Mercedes models use the M260/M264?
items.answer: The M260 (transverse) powers A‑Class, B‑Class, CLA, GLA, and GLB. The M264 (longitudinal) powers C‑Class, E‑Class, CLS, GLC, and G‑Class — the key difference is engine orientation.

items.question: How long does the M260/M264 last?
items.answer: With meticulous maintenance, 160,000–190,000 miles is achievable — but high‑mileage examples often require significant expenditure on cylinder head or timing chain work. The 48V mild-hybrid system can also introduce additional complexity.
